news.headline: ClearPoint Neuro (CLPT) surges 26.0%: Is this an indication of further gains?
news.summary: A Zacks momentum piece flagged CLPT's outsized rally, tying the move to sustained investor enthusiasm after partner uniQure's FDA progress on AMT-130 and rising earnings-estimate revisions. The note frames the stock as riding platform-validation momentum rather than a fresh operational catalyst, so continued gains hinge on the uniQure BLA timeline holding.

news.headline: ClearPoint Neuro CFO and CBO acquire ESPP shares via Form 4
news.summary: CFO Danilo D'Alessandro acquired 1,085 shares and CBO L. Jeremy Stigall acquired 1,781 shares (now holding 110,263) at $11.93 each under the January-June 2026 Employee Stock Purchase Plan window; CEO Joseph Burnett also picked up 1,361 ESPP shares the same day. The routine plan purchases are a mild alignment signal following the mid-June AMT-130 rally, though ESPP buys are lower-conviction than open-market purchases.

news.headline: ClearPoint Neuro CEO exercises options and sells 20,000 shares under 10b5-1 plan
news.summary: CEO Joseph Burnett exercised options at $2.50 and sold 20,000 shares on June 29 at a weighted-average price of $19.38 (about $387,600) under a pre-arranged 10b5-1 trading plan. The scheduled nature of the disposal tempers the read, but it is part of a string of CEO option-related sales into the post-AMT-130 rally, a mild governance watch-item against the otherwise bullish insider ESPP activity.

news.headline: Lake Street and B. Riley raise ClearPoint Neuro price targets to $30 (Buy)
news.summary: Lake Street (Frank Takkinen) reiterated Buy with a $30 target and B. Riley's Anderson Schock raised his target to $30 from $20, both framing ClearPoint as the primary picks-and-shovels supplier benefiting from uniQure's AMT-130 progress. The moves reflect growing analyst conviction after the FDA news but leave valuation heavily dependent on partner execution.

news.headline: uniQure plans Q3 2026 BLA for AMT-130 after FDA accepts 3-year data for accelerated approval
news.summary: In a Type B meeting the FDA agreed the 3-year Phase I/II analysis (showing a 75% slowing of disease progression at the high dose) can serve as the primary basis for a BLA under accelerated approval for AMT-130 in Huntington's disease, with submission planned for Q3 2026; uniQure is separately pursuing a UK MHRA marketing application in the same window. AMT-130 is delivered directly into the brain via ClearPoint's SmartFlow cannula and navigation platform, so CLPT benefits directly - the stock surged as much as 50% intraday, the single biggest catalyst of the period.

news.headline: ClearPoint Neuro enters 10-year focused-ultrasound partnership with Sungkyunkwan University, South Korea
news.summary: The decade-long collaboration, built with SONOCARELAB at Sungkyunkwan University, follows a successful preclinical proof-of-concept using focused ultrasound to open the blood-brain barrier for large-molecule delivery. It expands ClearPoint's drug-delivery ecosystem into focused ultrasound and deepens its footprint in Asia, a strategic long-dated bet with limited near-term revenue impact.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings (after market close)
catalysts.detail: Next quarterly report; watch organic growth, IRRAflow integration, cash burn and confirmation of the $52-56M FY guide. Date follows the historical mid-August cadence and is not yet formally confirmed by the company.

catalysts.label: uniQure AMT-130 BLA submission
catalysts.detail: uniQure plans to file the BLA for AMT-130 in Huntington's under accelerated approval in Q3 2026 (and a parallel UK MHRA marketing application); ClearPoint's SmartFlow cannula and navigation platform is the delivery system, making this the key partner milestone to watch.

catalysts.label: Potential FDA decision on AMT-130
catalysts.detail: Assuming a Q3 2026 filing and priority/rolling review, an FDA decision could arrive around mid-2027; approval of a first Huntington's gene therapy would establish recurring per-procedure delivery revenue for ClearPoint.

catalysts.label: Revenue guidance $52-56M
catalysts.detail: Management's full-year target implies continued double-digit growth; meeting or raising it at the Q2/Q3 prints is viewed as a share-price catalyst.

catalysts.label: Velocity Alpha drill / navigation rollout
catalysts.detail: Continued commercialization of the CE-marked, FDA-cleared Velocity Alpha MR high-speed surgical drill and navigation systems into new hospital sites to drive hardware and recurring consumable placements.

pulse: CLPT's setup remains driven almost entirely by partner uniQure's mid-June regulatory win: the FDA's acceptance of 3-year AMT-130 data for an accelerated-approval BLA (targeted Q3 2026) sent the stock up sharply and revived the picks-and-shovels bull case, with Lake Street and B. Riley both lifting targets to $30 and Zacks momentum coverage keeping the name in focus into July. The operating picture is still partner-dependent and unprofitable, so upside rests on gene therapies reaching market rather than standalone fundamentals, and insider signals are mixed - CFO/CBO/CEO ESPP buying against a scheduled CEO 10b5-1 option sale on June 29. With no fresh operational news since the May 27 South Korea focused-ultrasound partnership, the stock is now consolidating between partner headlines ahead of the ~Aug 11 Q2 print and confirmation of the actual uniQure BLA filing.
